hero.title: Strengthening Cancer Care Across Africa
hero.subtitle: Building capacity, raising awareness, and fostering partnerships for better cancer prevention, detection, and treatment.

about.title: Driven By Purpose And Impact
about.subtitle: Building a future where quality cancer care is accessible to all Africans through training, awareness, and partnerships.
about.whoWeAreText: Cancer Care Africa Foundation (CanCAF) is a pan-African non-profit organisation committed to transforming the landscape of cancer care across the African continent. We exist to strengthen cancer control systems, reduce disparities, and ensure that every person can access quality cancer prevention, early detection, treatment, survivorship, and compassionate care.
about.originTitle: Born From Global Recognition and African Excellence
about.originText1: CanCAF was inspired and birthed through the global spotlight that followed Naomi Oyoe Ohene Oti's historic achievement as the winner of the 2025 Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award. Her recognition on the world stage elevated the urgent need for stronger cancer care systems in Africa and ignited a new movement, one driven by leadership, compassion, and a commitment to equitable healthcare.
about.originText2: What began as a moment of individual excellence has evolved into a continental mission: to build a future where Africans no longer suffer disproportionately from preventable and treatable cancers.
about.originStoryTitle: The Award That Started It All
about.founderQuote: My vision is to transform cancer care across Africa, ensuring that no one suffers alone and that quality treatment is accessible to all.
about.purposeText1: Africa carries a growing cancer burden, yet access to early detection, diagnostic services, treatment technologies, and palliative care remains unequally distributed.
about.purposeText2: CanCAF was established to address these gaps and to champion African-led, sustainable solutions that reshape cancer care across the continent. We work across the cancer control continuum by bringing together systems, people, and strategic partnerships to drive lasting change.
about.missionText: Cancer Care Africa Foundation (CanCAF) is committed to strengthening cancer control systems through capacity building, awareness, advocacy, policy engagement, and strategic partnerships to improve prevention, early detection, treatment, and survivorship.
about.visionText: A future where every person in Africa has access to quality cancer prevention, early detection, treatment, and compassionate care.
about.advisoryBoardText: Our advisory board brings together distinguished leaders in oncology, public health, nursing, and healthcare policy from across Africa and beyond.
about.advisoryBoardComingSoon: Advisory board members will be announced soon.
about.whereWeWorkText: CanCAF operates across Africa through strategic partnerships with health institutions, governments, and organisations committed to improving cancer care. Our reach continues to expand as we work together to strengthen cancer control systems continent-wide.

values.compassionDesc: We serve with empathy, dignity, and respect.
values.equityDesc: We promote fair access to quality cancer care.
values.impactDesc: We are committed to lasting change.

programs.title: Transforming Cancer Care Across Africa
programs.subtitle: Strategic initiatives driving meaningful change in cancer prevention, detection, and treatment.
programs.introText: To fulfil our mission and advance our vision for a healthier Africa, CanCAF drives a focused portfolio of high-impact initiatives.
programs.capacityBuildingDesc: CanCAF provides training and professional development opportunities for healthcare providers and institutions to enhance cancer prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and care delivery. Our programs support workforce development and strengthen institutional capacity across Africa.
programs.awarenessDesc: We conduct community outreach and public education initiatives to increase awareness about cancer prevention, early detection, and treatment. Our awareness programs aim to reduce stigma and encourage timely health-seeking behaviour.
programs.earlyDetectionDesc: CanCAF supports initiatives that promote early diagnosis through screening education, referral pathways, and collaboration with healthcare facilities and stakeholders.
programs.advocacyDesc: We support evidence-informed advocacy and policy dialogue to strengthen cancer control strategies and improve access to affordable cancer services across Africa.
programs.partnershipsDesc: We work with governments, health institutions, civil society organisations, and international partners to advance comprehensive cancer control and strengthen health systems.
programs.flagshipDesc1: CanCAF is honoured to launch CGC-PON Africa: The Cancer Genetic Counselling Certificate Programme for Oncology Nurses, a flagship initiative made possible through the 2025 Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award won by Mrs. Naomi Oyoe Ohene Oti. Her global recognition did more than celebrate exceptional nursing leadership; it sparked a vision for how African nurses could be empowered to change the future of cancer care on the continent.
programs.flagshipDesc2: Guided by that vision, CanCAF serves as the driving vehicle, bringing together the right partners, resources, and purpose, while the West African Genetic Medicine Centre (WAGMC) leads the delivery of this specialised training. The programme is further supported by the Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award, whose grant has helped transform a moment of personal achievement into a continental movement.
programs.flagshipDesc3: CGCPON Africa is designed to uplift and equip oncology nurses across Africa with the knowledge, confidence, and specialist skills needed to support patients and families navigating the often-overwhelming world of cancer genetics. By strengthening nurses at the frontline, we help close critical gaps in early detection, personalised treatment, family risk assessment, and compassionate genetic guidance.
programs.flagshipDesc4: At its heart, this programme recognises a simple truth: when nurses are empowered, patients and families feel safer, better supported, and more hopeful. CGCPON Africa reflects our shared commitment to building a future where every person in Africa, regardless of where they live or what they earn, can receive thoughtful, informed, culturally grounded guidance as part of their cancer care journey.
programs.flagshipDesc5: This is more than training. It is a tribute, a catalyst, and a collective promise to make cancer care in Africa stronger, fairer, and more humane.
programs.courseOutlineSubtitle: Comprehensive curriculum covering all aspects of cancer genetic counselling

aboutSection.title: Building Stronger Cancer Care Systems
aboutSection.description: Cancer Care Africa Foundation (CanCAF) is a purpose-driven non-profit organization committed to strengthening cancer control systems across Africa.
aboutSection.missionText: To strengthen cancer control systems through capacity building, awareness, advocacy, and strategic partnerships.

flagshipProgram.title: Building Cancer Care Capacity
flagshipProgram.programSubtitle: Cancer Genetic Counselling Certificate Programme for Oncology Nurses
flagshipProgram.description: CanCAF in partnership with West African Genetic Medicine Centre (WAGMC) and in collaboration with Aster Guardian Global Nursing Award launches a pioneering programme equipping African oncology nurses with cancer genetic counselling skills.

cgcpOnAfrica.title: CGCPON Africa: Cancer Genetic Counselling Certificate Programme for Oncology Nurses
cgcpOnAfrica.subtitle: A pioneering virtual certificate programme equipping African oncology nurses with cancer genetic counselling skills. July - November 2025.
cgcpOnAfrica.aboutTitle: Bridging the Cancer Genetic Counselling Gap in Africa
cgcpOnAfrica.aboutDesc1: The Cancer Genetic Counselling Certificate Programme for Oncology Nurses in Africa (CGCPON Africa) is a landmark initiative addressing a critical gap: approximately 90% of African countries have no genetic counsellors. This virtual programme equips oncology nurses with foundational competencies in cancer genetic counselling.
cgcpOnAfrica.aboutDesc2: Made possible through grant funding from the 2025 Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award, won by Naomi Oyoe Ohene Oti, this programme represents a collaborative effort between the West African Genetic Medicine Centre (WAGMC), Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award, and CanCAF.
cgcpOnAfrica.whyMattersDesc: With the growing influence of precision oncology worldwide, there is a significant need to integrate genetic counselling into routine oncology care. However, the shortage of trained professionals in Africa presents a significant barrier. CGCPON Africa addresses this by training oncology nurses who are already embedded in cancer care systems.

skills.geneticsDesc: Understand hereditary cancer syndromes, genetic mutations, and their clinical implications.
skills.riskAssessmentDesc: Conduct comprehensive family history assessments and identify patients at increased genetic risk.
skills.counsellingDesc: Develop communication skills for discussing genetic risk, testing options, and results with patients.
skills.testingDesc: Guide patients through informed decision-making about genetic testing and its implications.
skills.precisionDesc: Understand how genetic information informs personalized cancer treatment decisions.
skills.ethicsDesc: Address ethical considerations and provide psychosocial support for patients and families.

cgcpOnAfrica.trainingDesc: Our programme combines expert-led theoretical modules with case-based learning and simulation practice, designed for working professionals across Africa.

training.module1Desc: Comprehensive theoretical modules delivered by genetic medicine specialists and experienced counsellors.
training.module2Desc: Real-world case studies exploring hereditary cancer syndromes and counselling scenarios.
training.module3Desc: Role-play sessions to develop and refine genetic counselling communication skills.
training.module4Desc: Ongoing guidance from experienced genetic counsellors throughout the programme.

cgcpOnAfrica.eligibilityDesc: We are seeking dedicated oncology nurses from across Africa who are committed to expanding genetic counselling services in their communities.

eligibility.registeredNurse: Registered nurse with valid nursing license in your country
eligibility.workingInOncology: Currently working in oncology or cancer care services
eligibility.institutionalSupport: Letter of support from your employing institution
eligibility.commitToService: Commitment to dedicate 10 hours monthly to genetic counselling services post-training

cgcpOnAfrica.commitmentDesc: Upon completing the CGCPON Africa programme, graduates will be expected to dedicate a minimum of 10 hours per month to providing cancer genetic counselling services within their institutions or communities.
cgcpOnAfrica.readyToApplyDesc: Join the pioneer cohort of oncology nurses building cancer genetic counselling capacity across Africa.
cgcpOnAfrica.partnersDesc: This programme is made possible through the collaboration of leading organizations committed to advancing genetic medicine in Africa.
cgcpOnAfrica.grantInfoDesc: This programme is funded through the 2025 Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award, recognizing excellence in nursing leadership and innovation.
cgcpOnAfrica.ctaTitle: Be Part of the Pioneer Cohort
cgcpOnAfrica.ctaDesc: Applications are now open for CGCPON Africa. Join us in building cancer genetic counselling capacity across the continent.

applicationForm.title: CGCP-ON Africa Application
applicationForm.subtitle: Complete the form below to apply for the training programme.
applicationForm.currentInstitutionPlaceholder: Name of your hospital or institution
applicationForm.departmentPlaceholder: e.g., Oncology Ward, Women's Health Unit
applicationForm.yearsOncologyExperience: Years of Experience in Oncology Nursing
applicationForm.oncologyTraining: Have you received any oncology-specific training or certification?
applicationForm.oncologyTrainingPlaceholder: If yes, please describe your oncology training...
applicationForm.priorGeneticsTraining: Have you had any prior training in cancer genetics or genetic counselling?
applicationForm.interestReason: Why are you interested in participating in this programme?
applicationForm.interestReasonPlaceholder: Describe your motivation for joining this programme... (max 200 words)
applicationForm.benefitDescription: How do you believe this programme will benefit your professional practice and your patients?
applicationForm.benefitDescriptionPlaceholder: Explain how this training will enhance your work... (max 200 words)
applicationForm.counselingExperience: Describe any previous experience you have in patient counselling, particularly in sensitive areas such as cancer diagnosis or genetic risk.
applicationForm.counselingExperiencePlaceholder: Share your relevant counselling experience... (max 200 words)
applicationForm.institutionSupportsParticipation: My institution supports my participation in this programme
applicationForm.canApplySkills: I confirm that I will be able to apply the skills learned in my workplace upon completion
applicationForm.canParticipateVirtually: Are you able to fully participate in the programme (virtual)?
applicationForm.hasReliableInternet: Do you have reliable internet access for virtual sessions?
applicationForm.supportingDocuments: Please ensure you have the following documents ready to upload:
applicationForm.declarationText: I certify that the information provided in this application is true and complete to the best of my knowledge. I understand that any false information may result in disqualification from the programme. I agree to abide by the programme's policies and code of conduct.
applicationForm.declarationAgree: I agree to the above declaration
applicationForm.signatureNote: Digital signature will be added upon submission
applicationForm.name: Name
applicationForm.successMessage: Thank you for applying to the CGCP-ON Africa programme. We have received your application and will review it carefully. You will receive a confirmation email shortly, and our team will contact you regarding the next steps.
